Sections:
  context
    Code-patrol scan on 2026-06-09 surfaced five small quality issues across src/. (1) sd search lacks plan-annotation parity with sd list and sd ready in human/plain output. (2) The plan-suffix branch in sd list and sd ready writes via process.stdout.write and bypasses the -q/--quiet flag enforced through src/output.ts printIssueOneLine. (3) src/log.ts resolveLevel() casts SEEDS_LOG_LEVEL to pino.Level without validation, so an unknown value crashes logger init at import time. (4) src/priority.ts parsePriority + isValidPriority accept fractional input ('2.5' -> 2 via Number.parseInt) while src/filter.ts parsePriorityToken rejects it with a regex guard; the same flag is policed inconsistently across commands. (5) src/store.ts appendIssue / appendTemplate / appendPlan mutate JSONL files without acquiring the lock themselves and trust callers to wrap them in withLock — the contract is enforced by convention but absent from the JSDoc. None of these changes touch a public CLI flag, command name, or JSON payload key.
  approach
    Land one small, focused PR per finding. Each PR adds or extends a co-located *.test.ts before changing behavior. No new dependencies, no module reorganization, no public-API signature changes. Inconsistencies are resolved by making the laxer call site match the stricter one (priority parsing) or by porting an existing helper (plan annotation in search). Quality gates (bun test, bun run lint, bun run typecheck, bun run check:size, bun run check:debt, bun run check:agents) must all pass on each PR. Per-step detail: STEP 1 — src/commands/list.ts default branch (~lines 145-158) and src/commands/ready.ts default branch (~lines 178-192) use `if (suffix) process.stdout.write(...) else printIssueOneLine(...)`; process.stdout.write skips the _quiet gate that printIssueOneLine respects. Fix: route the suffix-bearing line through an output.ts helper (e.g. extend printIssueOneLine to take an optional suffix, or add printIssueOneLineWithSuffix) so -q suppresses both branches. Add regression tests in list.test.ts and ready.test.ts using the existing test-harness capture. STEP 2 — src/commands/search.ts (~lines 135-165) calls issueJsonWithPlan for json mode but plain and default modes render via formatIssueOneLine without planLineSuffix, unlike list.ts and ready.ts. Port the same pattern: load planCtx (already imported elsewhere in the file or via loadPlanContext), build planForIssue, append planLineSuffix in plain and default. Add a search.test.ts case verifying a seed under an active plan renders the suffix. STEP 3 — src/log.ts resolveLevel() does `const explicit = process.env.SEEDS_LOG_LEVEL as pino.Level | undefined` — replace the bare cast with a check against pino.levels.values keys (trace/debug/info/warn/error/fatal/silent). Unknown values fall back to the SEEDS_DEBUG / info default. Add a log.test.ts case for an unknown level. STEP 4 — src/priority.ts parsePriority: after stripping the optional P/p prefix, validate the remaining substring matches /^\d+$/ before Number.parseInt; return NaN otherwise so callers raise PRIORITY_ERROR. Mirrors src/filter.ts parsePriorityToken. Extend priority.test.ts to cover '2.5', '2a', ' 2 ', 'P2.5', and confirm '2' and 'P2' still pass. STEP 5 — src/store.ts: add JSDoc to appendIssue, appendTemplate, appendPlan stating 'Caller must hold withLock(<correspondingPath>(seedsDir), …) — these helpers do not acquire the file lock themselves; concurrent writes without the lock can lose data.' No code change; documentation-drift fix. STEP 6 — release per .claude/commands/release.md once all five fixes are merged.

cd /workspace && git add -A && git commit -m "fix(list,ready): respect -q/--quiet in plan-suffix branch (seeds-6848)

The default-format branches in sd list and sd ready emitted the
plan-annotated issue line via process.stdout.write, which bypassed the
_quiet gate inside printIssueOneLine. With -q/--quiet set, issues
carrying a plan suffix still appeared on stdout while issues without a
plan correctly stayed silent.

Extend printIssueOneLine with an optional trailing suffix, and route
both default branches through it. The plain (--format plain) path and
the count line are unchanged — pre-existing behavior.

Also teach the in-process test harness to mirror src/index.ts: when
-q/--quiet is in argv, call setQuiet(true) before dispatch and
setQuiet(false) on cleanup. Without this, the harness silently skips
the quiet wiring that the real CLI applies in index.ts.

Tests:
- src/commands/list.test.ts: new describe block covering -q
  suppression of the plan-suffix line for both sd list and sd ready,
  plus a regression that the suffix is still emitted without -q.
